 {"id":"git_safety_guard-4km","title":"[PACK] email.mailgun - Mailgun Pack","description":"# Mailgun Email Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Mailgun API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### API Operations
- Domain deletion: `DELETE /v3/domains/{domain}`
- Route deletion: `DELETE /v3/routes/{id}`
- Credential deletion
- Mailing list deletion: `DELETE /v3/lists/{address}`
- Template deletion
- Webhook deletion

## Safe Patterns
- `GET /v3/domains`
- `GET /v3/routes`
- `GET /v3/stats`
- Domain listing
- Log retrieval
- Email sending (POST)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: email.* (git_safety_guard-62v)
- Keywords: mailgun
- Note: REST API based

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for domain deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for route deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for mailing list deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/email_mailgun.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Route deletion blocked

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-4o8","title":"Create documentation for heredoc detection feature","description":"## Objective

Create comprehensive documentation for the heredoc detection feature, covering user configuration, pattern authoring, and security considerations.

## Documentation Deliverables

### 1. User Guide Updates

#### README.md Updates

- Add heredoc scanning to feature list
- Document new command-line options (if any)
- Update configuration examples

#### Configuration Guide

- How to enable/disable heredoc scanning
- Language-specific configuration
- Performance tuning options
- Fallback behavior settings

### 2. Pattern Authoring Guide

New document: `docs/patterns.md`

#### Pattern Syntax

- tree-sitter query syntax (if using queries)
- ast-grep pattern syntax (if using ast-grep-core)
- Examples for each supported language

#### Adding New Patterns

- Step-by-step guide
- Testing requirements
- Performance considerations
- Review checklist

#### Pattern Library Reference

- Complete list of all patterns
- What each pattern detects
- Known limitations
- False positive/negative notes

### 3. Security Documentation

New document: `docs/security.md`

#### Threat Model

- Attack vectors heredoc detection addresses
- Attack vectors explicitly out of scope
- Assumptions and limitations

#### Bypass Considerations

- Known potential bypasses
- Why certain bypasses are accepted
- Defense in depth recommendations

#### Incident Response

- What to do if a command is wrongly blocked
- What to do if a dangerous command gets through
- How to report security issues

### 4. Developer Documentation

#### Architecture Overview

- Pipeline flow diagram
- Module responsibilities
- Data flow through heredoc analysis

#### API Documentation

- Internal Rust API documentation (rustdoc)
- Integration points for extending
- Error handling patterns

#### Contributing Guide Updates

- How to add new language support
- How to add new patterns
- Testing requirements for contributions

### 5. AGENTS.md Updates

Update the AI agent guidelines:

- New heredoc detection capabilities
- How to test heredoc patterns
- False positive handling guidance

## Documentation Quality Requirements

- All code examples must be tested
- All configuration examples must be valid
- Diagrams for complex flows
- Cross-references between related docs

## Acceptance Criteria

- Documentation is sufficient for a new user to:
  - understand what heredoc scanning does
  - configure enable/disable + timeouts
  - interpret a heredoc block message
  - use allowlisting/explain to resolve false positives
- Documentation clearly calls out:
  - fail-open behavior and why
  - performance budgets and limits
  - out-of-scope threats (to prevent misaligned expectations)

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-4yij","title":"[Explain] Add explanation field to DestructivePattern","description":"# Task: Add explanation field to DestructivePattern

## Parent Epic: Visual Explanations (git_safety_guard-r97e)

## Objective

Extend the DestructivePattern struct to include an optional explanation field that provides human-readable context about why the pattern exists and what could go wrong.

## Current DestructivePattern Structure

Located in src/packs/mod.rs, includes:
- name: String
- description: String  
- pattern/regex: compiled regex
- severity: Severity enum

## Proposed Addition

pub struct DestructivePattern {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    /// Detailed explanation of why this pattern is dangerous.
    /// Should explain consequences and suggest alternatives.
    pub explanation: Option<String>,
    
    /// Suggested safe alternatives or preview commands.
    pub suggestions: Option<Vec<Suggestion>>,
}

pub struct Suggestion {
    pub label: String,      // e.g., "Preview first"
    pub command: String,    // e.g., "git diff"
    pub description: String // e.g., "See what would be lost"
}

## Implementation Steps

1. Add explanation field to DestructivePattern in src/packs/mod.rs
2. Add Suggestion struct for structured alternatives
3. Update pattern construction macros if any
4. Ensure backward compatibility (field is Option)
5. Update any serialization (JSON output in explain)

## Backward Compatibility

Using Option<String> ensures existing patterns without explanations continue to work. Explanations are additive content.

## Acceptance Criteria

- DestructivePattern has explanation field
- Field is optional and defaults to None
- Existing patterns compile without changes
- JSON serialization includes explanation when present

## Estimated Effort: 0.5 days","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:06:59.174524696-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T01:14:51.307573932-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T01:14:51.307573932-05:00","close_reason":"Added explanation field to DestructivePattern struct with backward-compatible macro variants"}

## Parent Epic: Visual Explanations (git_safety_guard-r97e)

## Objective

Extend the DestructivePattern struct to include an optional explanation field that provides human-readable context about why the pattern exists and what could go wrong.

## Current DestructivePattern Structure

Located in src/packs/mod.rs, includes:
- name: String
- description: String  
- pattern/regex: compiled regex
- severity: Severity enum

## Proposed Addition

pub struct DestructivePattern {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    /// Detailed explanation of why this pattern is dangerous.
    /// Should explain consequences and suggest alternatives.
    pub explanation: Option<String>,
    
    /// Suggested safe alternatives or preview commands.
    pub suggestions: Option<Vec<Suggestion>>,
}

pub struct Suggestion {
    pub label: String,      // e.g., "Preview first"
    pub command: String,    // e.g., "git diff"
    pub description: String // e.g., "See what would be lost"
}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-53q","title":"E2E: explain/simulate/scan CLI flows","description":"## Objective

Add E2E coverage for non-hook CLI flows to ensure parity and stability.

- `dcg simulate` input formats are parsed end-to-end with realistic fixtures.

- Format outputs are validated with jq or schema checks.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz","title":"Auto-Suggested Allowlists from History","description":"# Epic: Auto-Suggested Allowlists from History

## Priority Score: 700/1000 (Third Priority)

## Background and Rationale

Manually writing allowlist patterns is tedious and error-prone. Users either give up and disable DCG, write overly broad patterns, or spend hours crafting precise patterns.

This feature analyzes the history database to identify commands that are repeatedly blocked but appear safe, then generates suggested allowlist entries with confidence scoring for user approval.

## Example Workflow

User runs: dcg suggest-allowlist

Output shows:
  HIGH CONFIDENCE (blocked 127 times, consistent pattern):
    Commands: npm run build, npm run test, npm run lint
    Suggested pattern: npm run (build|test|lint)
    Risk assessment: LOW (read-only npm scripts)
    Accept? [y/N]

## Technical Approach

1. Query history for denied commands grouped by pattern_triggered
2. Cluster similar commands using normalized edit distance
3. Generate regex patterns that match each cluster
4. Score confidence based on frequency, consistency, risk factors
5. Present interactive CLI for approval/rejection
6. On accept, append to user allowlist config

## Clustering Algorithm

- Normalize all commands
- Tokenize into (command, subcommand, flags, args)
- Group by (command, subcommand, flags)
- For each group, find common argument patterns
- Generate regex from pattern

## Risk Scoring Factors

- Command frequency (more = higher confidence)
- Argument consistency (similar args = higher confidence)
- Dangerous flags present (-f, --force = lower confidence)
- Path sensitivity (system paths = lower confidence)

## Key Files

- src/history/schema.rs - Ensure rule_id tracking
- New: src/suggest.rs - Clustering and pattern generation
- src/cli.rs - Add dcg suggest-allowlist command
- src/allowlist.rs - Config writing integration

## Success Criteria

- Suggestions generated from at least 10 denied commands
- Confidence tiers: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW with clear criteria
- Interactive approval with risk explanation
- Generated patterns are valid regex
- Config file updated atomically

## Dependencies

Requires rule_id tracking in history (see Rule Metrics epic)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:34.569738697-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:34.569738697-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5apz.1","title":"[Suggest] Risk scoring + explicit manual confirmation","description":"# Task: Risk scoring + explicit manual confirmation

## Purpose
Suggestions must never auto-write allowlist entries. Each suggestion should include
an explicit risk score and require manual confirmation before writing.

## Requirements
- Risk scoring independent from confidence scoring
- Risk categories: low / medium / high (deterministic rules)
- Interactive CLI must require explicit confirmation per entry
- Non-interactive mode outputs suggestions only (no writes)

## Implementation
- Add `risk_score` to suggestion model
- Define scoring rules (flags like `--force`, destructive verbs, system paths)
- Enforce confirmation in CLI flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- No allowlist entry is written without a user confirmation step
- Risk score is visible in both text and JSON outputs

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-joyb","title":"Command Telemetry: Configuration options","notes":"
---

## IMPORTANT: Default Configuration Change

**CHANGE REQUIRED**: Default `redaction_mode` should be `pattern` NOT `none` for security-by-default.

```toml
[telemetry]
enabled = false           # Opt-in
redaction_mode = "pattern"  # CHANGED from "none" - secure by default
retention_days = 90
max_size_mb = 500
```

Users who want raw commands must explicitly set `redaction_mode = "none"`.

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_default_config_values() {
    let config = TelemetryConfig::default();

    assert!(!config.enabled);                    // Off by default
    assert_eq!(config.redaction_mode, RedactionMode::Pattern);  // Secure default!
    assert_eq!(config.retention_days, 90);
    assert_eq!(config.max_size_mb, 500);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_from_toml() {
    let toml = r#"
        [telemetry]
        enabled = true
        redaction_mode = "full"
        retention_days = 30
    "#;

    let config: Config = toml::from_str(toml).unwrap();
    assert!(config.telemetry.enabled);
    assert_eq!(config.telemetry.redaction_mode, RedactionMode::Full);
    assert_eq!(config.telemetry.retention_days, 30);
}

#[test]
fn test_redaction_mode_parsing() {
    assert_eq!(RedactionMode::from_str("none").unwrap(), RedactionMode::None);
    assert_eq!(RedactionMode::from_str("pattern").unwrap(), RedactionMode::Pattern);
    assert_eq!(RedactionMode::from_str("full").unwrap(), RedactionMode::Full);
    assert!(RedactionMode::from_str("invalid").is_err());
}

#[test]
fn test_env_var_override() {
    std::env::set_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_ENABLED", "true");
    std::env::set_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_REDACTION_MODE", "full");

    let config = TelemetryConfig::from_env_and_file(None);
    assert!(config.enabled);
    assert_eq!(config.redaction_mode, RedactionMode::Full);

    std::env::remove_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_ENABLED");
    std::env::remove_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_REDACTION_MODE");
}

#[test]
fn test_database_path_expansion() {
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        database_path: Some("~/.config/dcg/telemetry.db".into()),
        ..Default::default()
    };

    let expanded = config.expanded_database_path();
    assert!(!expanded.to_string_lossy().contains("~"));
    assert!(expanded.to_string_lossy().contains("dcg"));
}

#[test]
fn test_retention_days_validation() {
    // Minimum retention
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        retention_days: 0,
        ..Default::default()
    };
    assert!(config.validate().is_err());

    // Maximum retention (prevent accidental huge values)
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        retention_days: 10000,
        ..Default::default()
    };
    assert!(config.validate().is_err() || config.retention_days == 10000);
}
```

### Integration Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_config_applied_to_telemetry_writer() {
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        enabled: true,
        redaction_mode: RedactionMode::Full,
        ..Default::default()
    };

    let db = TelemetryDb::open_in_memory().unwrap();
    let writer = TelemetryWriter::new(&db, &config);

    writer.log(&CommandEntry {
        command: "curl -H 'Bearer secret123'".into(),
        ..Default::default()
    });
    writer.flush();

    let stored = db.query_recent(1).unwrap().pop().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(stored.command, "[REDACTED]");
}
```

### E2E Tests

```bash
# Test default redaction is 'pattern' (secure by default)
CONFIG=$(dcg config show --json | jq -r '.telemetry.redaction_mode')
[[ "$CONFIG" == "pattern" ]] && echo "PASS: Secure default" || echo "WARN: Check default"

# Test config file is respected
cat > /tmp/dcg_test_config.toml <<EOF
[telemetry]
enabled = true
retention_days = 7
EOF

DCG_CONFIG=/tmp/dcg_test_config.toml dcg telemetry status | grep -q "7 days" && \
    echo "PASS: Config respected"
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T00:41:28.917288585-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T03:38:24.861624903-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-14T03:38:24.861624903-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-joyb","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-9asp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T01:24:16.356907695-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","title":"Span Highlighting for Blocked Commands","description":"# Epic: Span Highlighting for Blocked Commands

## Priority Score: 820/1000 (Highest Priority)

## Background and Rationale

This feature addresses the number one user pain point with security tools: Why was my command blocked?

When DCG blocks a command, users currently see which pattern matched, but not exactly WHAT part of their command triggered the match. This leads to user frustration, perception of DCG as arbitrary, time wasted debugging, and reduced trust.

### Example of Proposed Output

When a destructive command is denied, show caret-style highlighting:

  command --dangerous-flag /sensitive/path
                           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
                           Path targeting system file

## Technical Approach

The match span data ALREADY EXISTS in DCG evaluation pipeline. PatternMatch captures match info and regex find() returns byte positions. We just need to surface this in the output.

## Implementation Steps

1. Extend EvaluationResult to carry match span information
2. Create terminal formatter for caret-style highlighting with ANSI colors  
3. Integrate into hook output (stderr warning on denial)
4. Add to dcg test output for consistency

## Key Files

- src/evaluator.rs - Ensure match spans captured
- src/hook.rs - Integrate highlighting into denial output
- src/cli.rs - Update dcg test output
- New: src/highlight.rs or extend src/trace.rs

## Success Criteria

- Denied commands show exactly which substring triggered the match
- Works correctly with multi-byte UTF-8 characters
- Works for compound commands (multiple matches shown)
- Colors disabled gracefully when not in TTY
- No performance regression

## Cross-Model Validation

Both Claude Opus 4.5 and comparison model rated this 820/1000. Strong consensus on value.","notes":"Keep dependency separation: Visual Explanations epic should not depend on span capture/highlighting tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:03:51.357954793-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:16:42.362535647-05:00"}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.1","title":"[Span] Ensure match spans captured for all match sources","description":"# Task: Ensure match spans captured for all match sources

## Background
`matched_span` exists in `EvaluationResult`, but not all match paths populate it consistently.
Span highlighting will be unreliable unless every match source provides a byte span
(or an explicit fallback) in terms of the original command string.

## Scope
Cover **all** match sources:
- Pack regex matches (safe/destructive patterns)
- Legacy patterns (if any remain in evaluator paths)
- Heredoc/AST matches (inline script detection)

## Approach
- Audit match creation in `src/evaluator.rs` and ensure each code path sets:
  - `matched_span` (byte offsets)
  - `matched_text_preview` (already present; keep for fallback)
- For pack regex matches, use `regex.find()` from the selected engine to capture start/end.
- For legacy patterns, capture span from the legacy regex if used.
- For AST/heredoc matches, map extracted script offsets back to the original command when possible
  (use extraction metadata / heredoc boundaries). If mapping is impossible, store a structured reason
  and keep `matched_text_preview` as the fallback.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/heredoc.rs` / `src/ast_matcher.rs` (mapping helpers, if needed)
- `src/trace.rs` (optional: include span mapping reason in trace)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every deny/warn match source sets a usable `matched_span`, or records a clear fallback reason.
- Span is based on **original command**, not just normalized/sanitized forms.
- No performance regression on the hot path.

## Notes
This task is a prerequisite for reliable span highlighting. Keep it independent of the
Visual Explanations epic.

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.1","title":"[Span] Ensure match spans captured for all match sources","description":"# Task: Ensure match spans captured for all match sources

## Background
`matched_span` exists in `EvaluationResult`, but not all match paths populate it consistently.
Span highlighting will be unreliable unless every match source provides a byte span
(or an explicit fallback) in terms of the original command string.

## Scope
Cover **all** match sources:
- Pack regex matches (safe/destructive patterns)
- Legacy patterns (if any remain in evaluator paths)
- Heredoc/AST matches (inline script detection)

## Approach
- Audit match creation in `src/evaluator.rs` and ensure each code path sets:
  - `matched_span` (byte offsets)
  - `matched_text_preview` (already present; keep for fallback)
- For pack regex matches, use `regex.find()` from the selected engine to capture start/end.
- For legacy patterns, capture span from the legacy regex if used.
- For AST/heredoc matches, map extracted script offsets back to the original command when possible
  (use extraction metadata / heredoc boundaries). If mapping is impossible, store a structured reason
  and keep `matched_text_preview` as the fallback.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/heredoc.rs` / `src/ast_matcher.rs` (mapping helpers, if needed)
- `src/trace.rs` (optional: include span mapping reason in trace)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every deny/warn match source sets a usable `matched_span`, or records a clear fallback reason.
- Span is based on **original command**, not just normalized/sanitized forms.
- No performance regression on the hot path.

## Notes
This task is a prerequisite for reliable span highlighting. Keep it independent of the
Visual Explanations epic.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.2","title":"[Span] UTF-8 safe slicing + windowing for long commands","description":"# Task: UTF-8 safe slicing + windowing for long commands

## Background
Highlighting needs to work on arbitrary UTF-8 input. Naive slicing can panic
on non-boundary indices, and long commands can create unreadable output.

## Scope
- UTF-8 boundary-safe slicing around match spans
- Ellipsis/windowing for long commands (show match context)

## Approach
- Create a helper that:
  - Accepts `command`, `matched_span`, and `max_width`
  - Returns a **windowed** command string and adjusted caret span
  - Preserves valid UTF-8 boundaries
- Ensure the caret line aligns with the windowed output.
- Prefer no allocations on short commands.

## Files
- `src/hook.rs` (formatter helpers)
- Optional: new `src/highlight.rs` to keep formatting utilities isolated

## Acceptance Criteria
- No panics on multi-byte characters
- Long commands are truncated with `...` while keeping the match visible
- Caret span aligns with windowed output (TTY and non-TTY)

## Notes
This is a core utility for the formatter. Keep it small and testable.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:33.971333486-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:33.971333486-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:33.972586876-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.3","title":"[Span] Respect TTY/non-TTY for highlighting output","description":"# Task: Respect TTY/non-TTY for highlighting output

## Background
DCG already disables ANSI colors when stderr is not a TTY. Highlight output
must follow the same rule so non-TTY logs remain clean and machine-readable.

## Scope
- Ensure caret/highlight formatting is correct in both modes
- Avoid ANSI sequences when `stderr` is not a TTY or when `NO_COLOR` is set

## Approach
- Use existing color control in `hook::configure_colors()` and `colored::control`.
- Provide a non-color fallback format (same caret positioning, no ANSI).
- Add tests or assertions in `deny_output_tests` if feasible.

## Files
- `src/hook.rs`
- `src/logging.rs` (if formatting helpers live there)

## Acceptance Criteria
- TTY: colored output with caret highlighting
- non-TTY: plain output with caret highlighting, no ANSI escape codes
- `NO_COLOR` respected
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.704625444-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.704625444-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.705902509-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.4","title":"[Span] Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands","description":"# Task: Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands

## Background
DCG normalizes commands (wrapper stripping) and sanitizes safe spans.
If matching uses the normalized/sanitized string, highlight spans can drift
from the original command shown to users.

## Scope
- Ensure highlighted spans refer to the **original** command text
- Handle cases where normalization changes offsets

## Approach
- Track mapping between original and normalized/sanitized command when matches
  are computed on modified strings.
- If mapping is complex, compute match on the original command when safe
  (e.g., re-run regex on original for span only).
- Document fallback behavior when mapping is impossible.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/normalize.rs`
- `src/context.rs` / `src/trace.rs` (if mapping metadata stored)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Highlighted spans point to correct substring in original command
- No regression in decision logic (matching stays as before)
- Edge cases documented (e.g., when only preview is available)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e","title":"Visual Explanations for Pattern Blocks","description":"# Epic: Visual Explanations for Pattern Blocks

## Priority Score: 750/1000 (Second Highest)

## Background and Rationale

This feature complements span highlighting by answering the deeper question: WHY does this pattern exist?

Users need to understand not just WHAT triggered the block, but WHY it matters. This transforms DCG from an obstacle into a teacher, building trust and helping users make informed decisions about allowlisting.

## Example Output

When showing a denial, include contextual explanation:

  Pattern: git.reset-hard
  Why: git reset --hard destroys uncommitted changes permanently.
       There is no way to recover work that has not been committed.
  
  Suggestion: Use git stash to save changes first, or git reset --soft
              to preserve changes in the working directory.

## Technical Approach

1. Add optional explanation field to DestructivePattern struct
2. Write explanations for all core patterns (approximately 30-40 patterns)
3. Create suggestion templates for common remediation steps
4. Integrate into hook denial output alongside span highlighting
5. Surface in dcg explain output

## Key Files

- src/packs/mod.rs - Add explanation field to DestructivePattern
- src/packs/core/*.rs - Add explanations to each pattern
- src/hook.rs - Display explanations in denial output
- src/trace.rs - Include in explain output

## Content Strategy

Explanations should include:
- What the command does (destructive action)
- Why it is dangerous (consequences)
- What could go wrong (scenarios)
- Safe alternatives (suggestions)

## Success Criteria

- Every core pattern has an explanation
- Explanations are concise but informative (2-4 sentences)
- At least one safe alternative suggested per pattern
- Documentation links where applicable

## Synergy with Epic 1

This works together with span highlighting - users see WHAT matched and understand WHY it matters. The combination is more powerful than either alone.","notes":"All packs must receive explanations; tasks under this epic partition the workload but do not narrow scope. Epic is not complete until explanations exist for core + all non-core packs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:04:33.031740999-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:26:21.925415787-05:00"}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.1","title":"[Explain] Explanation content style guide","description":"# Task: Explanation content style guide

## Purpose
Provide a consistent writing standard for explanations so tone, length, and
structure are predictable and professional.

## Requirements
- Short, neutral, factual language
- Avoid scaremongering or moralizing
- 2–4 sentences max unless complexity requires more
- Include consequence + safer alternative (when applicable)
- Prefer deterministic guidance over subjective phrasing

## Deliverables
- A short style guide in `docs/explanations.md` (or similar)
- Examples of **good vs bad** explanation text
- Recommended structure template

## Acceptance Criteria
- Guide is concise and usable by anyone editing pack patterns
- Example explanations cover git + filesystem + one non-core pack

## Notes
This guide is a dependency for writing large volumes of explanation text.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.2","title":"[Explain] Fallback explanation for patterns without explicit text","description":"# Task: Fallback explanation for patterns without explicit text

## Purpose
Not all patterns will have bespoke explanations immediately. Provide a safe,
clear fallback to avoid empty explanation sections and keep UX consistent.

## Requirements
- Fallback should mention the matched pack + pattern name
- Explain that the command matched a destructive pattern and point to
  `dcg explain` or docs for details
- Must be neutral and concise

## Implementation
- Add fallback formatting in hook/CLI output when explanation is missing
- Expose fallback in explain JSON output for consistent tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- No empty explanation sections in any output
- Fallback never blocks output rendering
- Works with both TTY and non-TTY formats
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:18:42.102332805-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:18:42.102332805-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-r97e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:18:42.103625729-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:19:41.247889213-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.3","title":"[Explain] Make explanation optional + preserve backward compatibility","description":"# Task: Make explanation optional + preserve backward compatibility

## Purpose
Ensure adding explanations does **not** break existing packs or call sites.
Patterns should function normally even without an explanation field.

## Requirements
- Explanation field is optional in `DestructivePattern`
- Default behavior: no compile/runtime failure if missing
- Output layer uses fallback explanation when missing

## Implementation
- Update `DestructivePattern` to carry `Option<&'static str>` explanation
- Update constructors/macros to allow absence
- Ensure serialization/tracing handles None cleanly

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing packs compile without modification
- Explanation output renders even when field missing (via fallback task)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.4","title":"[Explain] Write explanations for remaining (long-tail) packs","description":"# Task: Write explanations for remaining non-core packs (Batch B)

## Purpose
Complete explanation coverage by adding text for **all remaining** non-core packs
not covered in Batch A. This is required for full epic completion.

## Packs to Cover
Everything not handled by:
- core.git / core.filesystem
- non-core Batch A task

Examples include:
- monitoring.*
- messaging.*
- cdn.*
- platform.*
- email.*
- featureflags.*
- loadbalancer.*
- dns.*
- secrets.*
- storage.*
- remote.*
- apigateway.*
- cicd.*
- payment.*
- search.*
- system.* (if not covered elsewhere)

## Requirements
- Use the explanation style guide
- Include at least one safer alternative or mitigation tip
- Keep explanations short and neutral

## Acceptance Criteria
- All remaining packs have explanations for destructive rules
- No pack left without explanation text

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.5","title":"[Explain] Unit tests for explanation rendering + fallback + JSON","description":"# Task: Unit tests for explanation rendering + fallback + JSON

## Purpose
Guarantee explanations render correctly across hook/CLI/JSON outputs and that
fallback text is used when explicit explanations are missing.

## Requirements
- Unit tests for explanation formatting in hook output
- Tests verifying fallback usage when explanation is None
- Tests for explain JSON fields + schema version

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover both TTY and non-TTY formatting paths
- JSON output includes explanation fields when present
- Fallback is stable and non-empty when missing

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-r97e.6","title":"[Explain] E2E tests for explanation output + verbose logs","description":"# Task: E2E tests for explanation output + verbose logs

## Purpose
Validate end-to-end behavior: explanations show up in hook output and CLI explain
with detailed logs for failures.

## Requirements
- Extend E2E script to validate explanation text for known blocked commands
- Include verbose logging of stderr/stdout on failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests pass in both TTY and non-TTY contexts
- Logs include full rendered explanation section
